title: Incorrect simulation result due to some optimizations
body:
The following is the problematic code `test.v`. I’ve tried my best to minimize it, but it seems the error only occurs during optimization of relatively complex expressions, so it still appears a bit long.

```verilog
`timescale 1ns/1ps
`define stop $stop
`define checkd(gotv, expv) \
do if ((gotv) !== (expv)) begin \
  $write("%%Error: %s:%0d:  got=%0d exp=%0d\n", `__FILE__, `__LINE__, (gotv), (expv)); \
  `stop; \
end while(0);
module top (out20);

reg in0;
reg signed [7:0] in4;
wire [1:0] wire_0;
output wire out20;

assign wire_0 = in4[0:0] ? ({{7{in4[3:1]}}, 12'd201} & 2'h2) : (!(in0) >> 9'b1111);
assign out20 = wire_0[0:0];

initial begin
  #10;
  in4 = 16'b1111_1110;
  in0 = 7'b0;
  #10;
  `checkd(out20, '0);
  $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
  $finish;
end

endmodule
```

You can reproduce the issue with the following commands:

```
verilator --binary -Wno-lint --timing test.v
./obj_dir/Vtest
```

Verilator output:

```
%Error: test.v:22:  got=1 exp=0
%Error: test.v:22: Verilog $stop
Aborting...
Aborted (core dumped)
```

However, Icarus Verilog completes successfully with `*-* All Finished *-*`. 
The value of `out20` is `0`, because `in4 = 16'b1111_1110`, so `in4[0:0] = 0`, which causes `wire_0 = (!(in0) >> 9'b1111)`, resulting in `wire_0 = 0`, hence `out20 = 0`.

During local debugging, I found that enabling the following options makes Verilator produce the correct output:

```
-fno-dfg-peephole
-fno-dfg
-fno-dfg-post-inline
-fno-const-bit-op-tree
-fno-dfg-pre-inline 
-fno-gate
```

It seems to be related to optimization, but I'm not familiar with Verilator's optimization mechanisms, so it's difficult for me to debug further.

### System details:

* Verilator version: Verilator 5.037 devel rev v5.036-64-g2f6ecd185
* OS: Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS (Jammy), Kernel/Release: 22.04
